claim.content:
For a Fermi liquid with Fermi wave vector k_F (Å^{-1}), define a dimensionless wave-vector-dependent effective mass m^*(k) (measured in units of the bare 3He mass m_3) by choosing a background mass parameter m_0 ≥ 1 and a half-width fraction f > 0, defining the symmetric interval {k: |k−k_F| < f k_F}, setting m^*(k)=m_0 for |k−k_F| ≥ f k_F, and for |k−k_F| < f k_F setting
      $$
      m^{*}(k)=m_{0}+(m^{*}(k_{F})-m_{0})\frac{1+\cos\big(\frac{(k-k_{F})\pi}{f k_{F}}\big)}{2},
      $$
      with the Fermi-surface value specified as m^*(k_F)=2.8. This prescription provides a single smooth cosine interpolation between m_0 and m^*(k_F) over the interval of half-width f k_F and is used as the representation of the effective-mass enhancement in subsequent numerical integrations.
